codeReview.summary: The reviewed worker session does not satisfy the feature contract because its cited commit only covers typed error classification. The current repository now has a canonical explicit-id primitive and Repo::save delegation, but those required behaviors are not owned by the reviewed feature commit.

issues.description: The canonical explicit-id helper exists in the current tree at persist_explicit_id_primitive, but the reviewed feature-owned commit 665414a62b2bc3e31baa9e00398286dc17a62ec5 does not introduce or modify it. git show 665414a changes only core/src/error.rs, core/src/lib.rs, and core/tests/integration_db.rs, so the shared low-level explicit-id primitive is not auditable from the cited implementation artifact.

The feature contract explicitly requires Repo::save to delegate explicit-id writes through the same primitive as create_at/upsert_at, but the reviewed commit 665414a does not touch Repo::save. That delegation appears only later at core/src/repository/mod.rs:659-661 in commit c11c0f9, so the worker handoff points at the wrong commit for the claimed behavior.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-library-worker procedure does not clearly require workers to prove that the feature-owned commit actually contains the scoped runtime seam when they switch into verification-only mode against an authoritative dirty baseline.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: C:\Users\admin\appdb\.factory\skills\rust-library-worker\SKILL.md step 16 requires one canonical explicit-id primitive, but session 02aa3764-624d-4377-b6a5-c9bcfbce3990 handed off commit 665414a62b2bc3e31baa9e00398286dc17a62ec5 without any repository edit. git show --stat 665414a lists only core/src/error.rs, core/src/lib.rs, and core/tests/integration_db.rs, while the required Repo::save delegation lands later in commit c11c0f9.

summary: Fail: the reviewed session cites commit 665414a as implementing the explicit-id persistence primitive feature, but that commit only adopts typed DB error classification. The actual Repo::save delegation to the shared explicit-id primitive landed later in commit c11c0f9, so this feature submission remains an ownership/evidence mismatch.
